content.full_text:
April 24, 1991

Mr. Scott Hallabrin, Acting General Counsel  
Fair Political Practices Commission  
Ste. 800  
428 J Street  
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Mr. Hallabrin:

Ms. Linda Moureaux of your offices advised me to write and explain our distribution of materials regarding the Quincentennial.

Last November I became aware of two charts commissioned by the Sears Company: "The Era of Frontiers and Settlements," and "The Era of Explorations,"---An Hispanic History Collection. I contacted the Vice-President of Sears in Chicago, Mr. Rick Jones, and asked if I could present these materials to members of the Legislature as an informational and educational service.

The Sears Company sent me 500 copies in December.

Last week I sent one copy of each chart to each member of the Senate and Assembly, with an attached "For Your Information" from Senator Mello.

It was only after the materials were delivered that I realized these materials might be reportable, and I then called your office to ensure that everything was done in the proper manner.

I have sent copies of the collection to Ms. Moureaux and have contacted Sears to give me a value on the set. I will forward that to you as soon as I hear back from Chicago.

I can assure you that I was acting under the assumption that this set of materials was informational in nature.

Sincerely,

Kevin J. LaGraff
